description: Labelled clause test suite for LeaseGuard accuracy evaluation. 45 Ontario residential lease clauses with known ground-truth outputs. V3 adds vital services cutoff, quiet enjoyment violation, assignment prohibition, unlawful renewal obligation, multiple rent increases, service reduction without rent decrease, and retaliation/coercion — 7 new violation types with 15 new test cases (7 unenforceable + 7 compliant guards + 1 edge case).
ground_truth_basis: Ontario Residential Tenancies Act, 2006 (RTA), as currently in force

test_cases.description: Entry without notice — clearly unenforceable
test_cases.clause_text: The landlord may enter the unit at any time without notice for inspection purposes.

mock_statutes.text: 26(1) A landlord may enter a rental unit only in accordance with section 26 or 27. A landlord may enter a rental unit under clause (1)(b) without written notice if the notice has been waived in writing by the tenant, or under clause (1)(c) if before entering, the landlord informs or makes a reasonable effort to inform the tenant of the landlord's intention to enter not less than 24 hours before the time of entry. A landlord may enter a rental unit without notice in cases of emergency or if the tenant consents to the entry at the time of entry.

27(1) A landlord may enter a rental unit in accordance with written notice given to the tenant at least 24 hours before the time of entry under the following circumstances. (3) A notice under subsection (1) or (2) shall specify the reason for entry and shall be given in a form approved by the Board.

test_cases.description: Entry with vague 'reasonable notice' — not 24h written notice
test_cases.clause_text: The landlord may enter the rental unit after providing reasonable notice to the tenant.

mock_statutes.text: 27(1) A landlord may enter a rental unit in accordance with written notice given to the tenant at least 24 hours before the time of entry. The notice must be in writing and must specify the reason for entry and the time of entry. Oral notice or vague notice is not sufficient to satisfy the requirements of this section.

26 A landlord may enter a rental unit without written notice only in cases of emergency. In all other cases, at least 24 hours written notice is required. A landlord who enters without proper written notice violates the tenant's right to privacy under section 25.

test_cases.description: Excessive security deposit — three months rent
test_cases.clause_text: The tenant shall pay a security deposit of $6,600, equal to three months' rent, prior to occupancy.

mock_statutes.text: 105(1) A landlord shall not require or receive from a tenant any security deposit except a rent deposit that satisfies the conditions set out in section 106. (2) The maximum amount of a rent deposit that a landlord may require or receive is the lesser of the amount of one month's rent or the amount of rent for one rental period. Requiring a deposit greater than one month's rent is void and unenforceable.

106(1) A landlord may require a tenant to pay a rent deposit with respect to a tenancy if the landlord does so before entering into the tenancy agreement. (2) The amount of a rent deposit shall not be more than the lesser of the amount of one month's rent or the amount of rent for one rental period. A deposit exceeding this limit must be returned to the tenant.

test_cases.description: Non-refundable damage deposit — prohibited under RTA
test_cases.clause_text: The tenant shall pay a non-refundable damage deposit of $500 upon signing this agreement.

mock_statutes.text: 105(1) A landlord shall not require or receive from a tenant any security deposit except a rent deposit as described in section 106. Any amount collected as a damage deposit, pet deposit, key deposit (except as permitted under section 105(2)), or non-refundable deposit is prohibited. Such amounts are void and the landlord must return them to the tenant.

test_cases.description: Unlimited rent increase with only 30 days notice
test_cases.clause_text: The landlord may increase rent by any amount at any time with 30 days written notice to the tenant.

mock_statutes.text: 116(1) A landlord shall not increase the rent charged to a tenant for a rental unit without first giving the tenant at least 90 days written notice of the landlord's intention to do so. (2) A notice under subsection (1) is void if it was given while a previous notice under that subsection given to the same tenant was still in effect.

120(1) No landlord shall charge rent for a rental unit in an amount that is greater than the lawful rent permitted under this Part. The maximum rent increase is set by the annual Rent Increase Guideline published by the Ministry. A landlord may not increase rent by an amount that exceeds the Guideline without an order from the Landlord and Tenant Board.

test_cases.description: All maintenance offloaded to tenant — contradicts s.20
test_cases.clause_text: The tenant is solely responsible for all repairs and maintenance of the rental unit, including structural repairs, appliances, plumbing, and electrical systems.

mock_statutes.text: 20(1) A landlord is responsible for providing and maintaining a residential complex, including the rental units in it, in a good state of repair and fit for habitation and for complying with health, safety, housing and maintenance standards. (2) Subsection (1) applies even if the tenant was aware of a state of non-repair or a contravention of a standard before entering into the tenancy agreement. A landlord cannot contract out of this obligation.

33 The tenant is responsible for ordinary cleanliness of the rental unit, except to the extent that the tenancy agreement requires the landlord to clean it. The tenant is responsible for repair of damage to the rental unit or the residential complex caused by the wilful or negligent conduct of the tenant, another occupant of the rental unit or a person permitted in the residential complex by the tenant.

test_cases.description: Full waiver of RTA rights — void under s.3
test_cases.clause_text: The tenant hereby waives all rights under the Residential Tenancies Act, including the right to file applications at the Landlord and Tenant Board.

mock_statutes.text: 3 Subject to subsection 194(2), a provision in a tenancy agreement, or in any agreement relating to a tenancy, that is inconsistent with this Act or the regulations is void. 2006, c. 17, s. 3. A tenant cannot waive rights granted by the Act. Any clause purporting to do so is unenforceable.

4 Subject to sections 194 and 195, a landlord or tenant may not waive a requirement of this Act or the regulations. Any purported waiver of rights under the Residential Tenancies Act, including the right to apply to the Landlord and Tenant Board, is void and of no effect. 2006, c. 17, s. 4.

test_cases.description: Daily late fee — prohibited additional charge
test_cases.clause_text: The tenant shall pay a late fee of $50 for each day rent remains unpaid after the first of the month.

mock_statutes.text: 134(1) Unless otherwise prescribed, no landlord shall, directly or indirectly, with respect to any rental unit charge or collect any amount of money, including a fee, fine, penalty, or service charge, other than the rent charged for the rental unit. A daily late fee is an additional charge and is therefore prohibited.

test_cases.description: Compliant entry clause with 24h written notice [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The Landlord may enter the rental unit only in accordance with the Residential Tenancies Act, requiring 24-hour written notice except in cases of emergency or with the Tenant's written consent.

mock_statutes.text: 27(1) A landlord may enter a rental unit in accordance with written notice given to the tenant at least 24 hours before the time of entry. The notice must specify the reason and time of entry.

26 A landlord may enter a rental unit without written notice in cases of emergency or if the tenant consents to the entry at the time of entry. The landlord shall give at least 24 hours written notice in all other circumstances.

test_cases.description: Compliant last-month rent deposit [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The Tenant shall provide a rent deposit equal to one month's rent ($2,200) to be held as a last month's rent deposit and applied to the last month of the tenancy, in accordance with the Residential Tenancies Act.

mock_statutes.text: 106(1) A landlord may require a tenant to pay a rent deposit with respect to a tenancy before entering into the tenancy agreement. (2) The amount of a rent deposit shall not be more than the lesser of the amount of one month's rent or the amount of rent for one rental period. The deposit is applied to the last rental period.

test_cases.description: Standard rent payment clause [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The tenant shall pay rent of $2,200 on the first day of each month.

mock_statutes.text: 12(1) Every written tenancy agreement entered into on or after June 17, 1998 shall set out the legal name and address of the landlord to whom rent is to be paid. A tenancy agreement shall specify the amount of rent and the day on which it is due. Standard rent payment clauses specifying an amount and due date are compliant.

test_cases.description: RTA-compliant rent increase with guideline and 90 days notice [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The landlord may increase rent in accordance with the Ontario Rent Increase Guideline, with 90 days written notice to the tenant.

mock_statutes.text: 116(1) A landlord shall not increase the rent charged to a tenant without first giving the tenant at least 90 days written notice. A rent increase that complies with the Rent Increase Guideline and provides 90 days notice is lawful.

test_cases.description: Cleanliness obligation only — compliant [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The tenant shall keep the rental unit in a clean and sanitary condition, ordinary wear and tear excepted.

mock_statutes.text: 33 The tenant is responsible for ordinary cleanliness of the rental unit, except to the extent that the tenancy agreement requires the landlord to clean it. Requiring a tenant to maintain cleanliness is compliant with the RTA.

test_cases.description: No smoking clause — standard boilerplate [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: No smoking or vaping is permitted in the rental unit or in any common areas of the residential complex.

test_cases.description: Compliant entry with emergency exception [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The landlord may enter the rental unit with 24 hours written notice, or without notice in the event of an emergency requiring immediate access.

mock_statutes.text: 26 A landlord may enter a rental unit without written notice in cases of emergency. In all other circumstances, the landlord shall give the tenant at least 24 hours written notice before entry. A clause permitting 24-hour written notice plus emergency exception is compliant with the RTA.

test_cases.description: Lease-break penalty fee — void under RTA s.37/s.134
test_cases.clause_text: If the Tenant vacates or terminates the tenancy before the end of the fixed term, the Tenant shall pay a lease-break fee equal to three months' rent as liquidated damages.

mock_statutes.text: 37(1) A tenancy may be terminated only in accordance with this Act. A tenancy agreement cannot impose a financial penalty, lease-break fee, or liquidated damages clause for early termination — the Act provides the exclusive mechanism for ending a tenancy. Any such fee is void under section 3 of the Act.

134(1) No landlord shall, directly or indirectly, with respect to any rental unit charge or collect any amount of money, including a fee, fine, or penalty, other than rent. A lease-break fee or early termination penalty is an additional charge prohibited by this section.

test_cases.description: Surveillance cameras inside rental unit — privacy violation
test_cases.clause_text: The Landlord reserves the right to install and maintain security cameras inside the rental unit and common areas to monitor the premises at any time for security purposes.

mock_statutes.text: 28 A tenant is entitled to exclusive possession of the rental unit and to reasonable enjoyment of the unit. The installation of monitoring or surveillance devices inside a rental unit by the landlord violates the tenant's right to privacy and is prohibited. The tenant has a right to privacy in the rental unit that the landlord cannot unilaterally override.

test_cases.description: Required post-dated cheques — prohibited under RTA s.108
test_cases.clause_text: The Tenant must provide 12 post-dated cheques upon signing this agreement to cover the full year's rent payments.

mock_statutes.text: 108(1) A landlord shall not require a tenant to provide post-dated cheques or other negotiable instruments, or to permit automatic debiting of a bank account, as a condition of entering into a tenancy agreement or as a condition of continuing to rent the rental unit. Any tenancy agreement term requiring post-dated cheques is void.

test_cases.description: Per-night guest surcharge — prohibited additional charge under s.134
test_cases.clause_text: Guests may not stay more than 3 consecutive nights. Any guest who stays beyond this limit will be charged an additional $50 per night, billed directly to the tenant.

mock_statutes.text: 134(1) No landlord shall, directly or indirectly, with respect to any rental unit, charge or collect any amount of money, including a fee, fine, penalty, or service charge, other than the rent charged for the rental unit. A surcharge per guest or per night for guest stays is an additional charge prohibited by this section.

test_cases.description: Assignment/sublet administrative fee — prohibited under RTA s.97
test_cases.clause_text: The Tenant shall pay an administrative fee of $750 to the Landlord upon submitting any request to assign the tenancy or sublet the rental unit.

mock_statutes.text: 97(1) A tenant may assign a rental unit to another person with the consent of the landlord. (3) A landlord shall not charge a fee for consenting to an assignment or subletting, or for processing a request to assign or sublet. Any such fee is prohibited and void. The landlord's sole remedy upon refusal is to allow the tenant to terminate the tenancy.

test_cases.description: Self-help eviction — change locks on non-payment
test_cases.clause_text: If the Tenant fails to pay rent within 5 days of the due date, the Landlord may change the locks on the rental unit and remove the Tenant's belongings from the premises without further notice.

mock_statutes.text: 19 A landlord shall not seize a tenant's property for default in the payment of rent. A landlord shall not change the locking system on a door giving entry to a rental unit during the tenant's occupancy without providing replacement keys. Self-help eviction by lock change or removal of belongings is illegal.

59(1) If a tenant has not paid rent lawfully owing under a tenancy agreement, the landlord may give the tenant a notice of termination of the tenancy effective not earlier than the 14th day after the notice is given if the rent is paid monthly. The N4 notice process is the landlord's exclusive remedy for non-payment of rent. Changing locks or removing belongings is not permitted.

test_cases.description: Pet fine per occurrence — void no-pet + prohibited penalty under s.14/s.134
test_cases.clause_text: Pets are strictly prohibited in the rental unit. Any tenant found to have a pet on the premises will be fined $500 per occurrence.

mock_statutes.text: 14 A provision in a tenancy agreement prohibiting the presence of animals in or about the residential complex is void. Because the no-pet provision is void, any financial penalty, fine, or eviction threat based on having a pet is also unenforceable.

134(1) No landlord shall charge or collect any amount other than rent. A fine per occurrence for having a pet is an additional charge prohibited under this section, in addition to the no-pet provision being void under section 14.

test_cases.description: Rent abatement waiver — void under RTA s.3/s.82
test_cases.clause_text: The Tenant hereby waives any right to a rent abatement, rent reduction, or refund of rent under any circumstances whatsoever.

mock_statutes.text: 3(1) This Act applies despite any agreement or waiver to the contrary. A provision in a tenancy agreement that waives a tenant's right to a rent abatement, rent reduction, or any other remedy under this Act is void. The tenant cannot contract out of rights granted by the Act.

82(1) At a hearing of an application made by a landlord under section 69, a tenant may raise any issue that could be the subject of an application made by the tenant under this Act, including a claim for a rent abatement. Any waiver of this right is void under section 3 of the Act.

test_cases.description: Compliant tenant wilful/negligent damage responsibility [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The Tenant is responsible for the repair of damage caused by the wilful or negligent conduct of the Tenant, another occupant of the rental unit, or any person permitted in the residential complex by the Tenant.

mock_statutes.text: 34 The tenant is responsible for the repair of undue damage to the rental unit or residential complex caused by the wilful or negligent conduct of the tenant, another occupant of the rental unit or a person permitted in the residential complex by the tenant. This is the standard statement of tenant damage responsibility and is compliant with the RTA.

test_cases.description: Compliant subletting with landlord consent clause [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The Tenant may sublet the rental unit or assign the tenancy only with the prior written consent of the Landlord. The Landlord shall not arbitrarily or unreasonably withhold consent, in accordance with the Residential Tenancies Act.

mock_statutes.text: 97(1) A tenant may assign a rental unit to another person with the consent of the landlord. A landlord shall not arbitrarily or unreasonably withhold consent to an assignment. A clause permitting subletting with landlord consent, where consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, is compliant with the RTA.

test_cases.description: 48-hour written notice entry — compliant (exceeds 24h minimum) [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The Landlord will provide the Tenant with at least 48 hours written notice before entering the rental unit, specifying the reason and time of entry.

mock_statutes.text: 27(1) A landlord may enter a rental unit in accordance with written notice given to the tenant at least 24 hours before the time of entry. Any notice period of 24 hours or more that is in writing and specifies the reason and time of entry satisfies this requirement. 48 hours written notice is compliant.

test_cases.description: RTA-deferring landlord maintenance obligation [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The Landlord shall maintain the rental unit and residential complex in a good state of repair and fit for habitation, as required by the Residential Tenancies Act, 2006.

mock_statutes.text: 20(1) A landlord is responsible for providing and maintaining a residential complex in a good state of repair and fit for habitation. A clause that restates this landlord obligation is compliant with the RTA.

test_cases.description: Standard fixed-term tenancy clause [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: This tenancy agreement commences on July 1, 2024 and is for a fixed term of one year, ending June 30, 2025, at which point it converts to a monthly tenancy unless either party provides notice to terminate.

test_cases.description: Compliant utilities responsibility clause [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The Tenant shall pay for hydro and natural gas utilities supplied to the rental unit directly to the utility providers. The Landlord is responsible for water, sewage, and municipal charges.

test_cases.description: Compliant parking clause with optional add-on [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: One parking space is included with the rental unit at no additional charge. Additional parking spaces may be made available at $100 per month upon separate written agreement.

test_cases.description: Tenant responsible for vital services (heat/hydro) — void under RTA s.29
test_cases.clause_text: The Tenant is solely responsible for ensuring that all utilities including heat, hydro, and water remain connected and active at all times during the tenancy. The Landlord shall not be liable if services are disconnected due to non-payment by the Tenant.

mock_statutes.text: 29(1) A landlord shall not cause the supply of a vital service, care service or food to a tenant to be withheld or interrupted. (2) In this section, 'vital service' means hot or cold water, fuel, electricity, natural gas or heat. A landlord cannot transfer the duty to maintain vital services to the tenant. Any clause purporting to do so is void.

30(1) If a landlord withholds or interrupts the supply of a vital service contrary to section 29, a tenant may apply to the Board for an order. (2) The Board may issue an order requiring the landlord to restore the vital service immediately. The landlord's obligation is ongoing and non-delegable.

test_cases.description: Heat and water included in rent — compliant vital services [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The following utilities are included in the monthly rent: heat, hot and cold water, and sewage. The Tenant is responsible for hydro and internet, payable directly to the service providers.

mock_statutes.text: 29(1) A landlord shall not cause the supply of a vital service to be withheld or interrupted. A clause specifying that certain utilities are included in rent and others are paid separately by the tenant to utility providers is compliant — it clarifies responsibility without shifting the landlord's core obligation.

test_cases.description: Monthly inspection clause — substantial interference with quiet enjoyment
test_cases.clause_text: The Landlord reserves the right to conduct monthly inspections of the rental unit at any time during normal business hours with 24 hours notice.

mock_statutes.section_title: Landlord not to interfere with reasonable enjoyment
mock_statutes.text: 22 A landlord shall not at any time during a tenant's occupancy of a rental unit and before the day on which an order evicting the tenant is executed substantially interfere with the reasonable enjoyment of the rental unit or the residential complex in which it is located for all usual purposes by the tenant or members of the tenant's household. Regular monthly inspections beyond what is reasonably necessary substantially interfere with the tenant's quiet enjoyment.

test_cases.description: Annual inspection with 24h written notice — compliant [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The Landlord may conduct one annual inspection of the rental unit for maintenance purposes. The Landlord will provide at least 24 hours written notice specifying the date and time of the inspection (between 8 a.m. and 8 p.m.).

mock_statutes.text: 27(1) A landlord may enter a rental unit in accordance with written notice given to the tenant at least 24 hours before the time of entry between 8 a.m. and 8 p.m. An occasional annual inspection with proper 24-hour written notice is compliant with this section.

test_cases.description: Complete ban on subletting with no consent pathway — void under RTA s.95
test_cases.clause_text: Subletting and assignment of this tenancy is strictly prohibited under all circumstances. The Tenant may not transfer, sublet, or assign the rental unit or any rights under this agreement to any other person.

mock_statutes.text: 95(1) A tenant may assign a rental unit to another person with the consent of the landlord. (5) A landlord shall not arbitrarily or unreasonably withhold consent to an assignment. A complete prohibition on subletting or assignment with no consent pathway is void under this section — the landlord may require consent but cannot ban the process entirely.

test_cases.description: Subletting requires written consent — compliant [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The Tenant may sublet the rental unit or assign the tenancy only with the prior written consent of the Landlord. The Landlord shall not arbitrarily or unreasonably withhold such consent, in accordance with the Residential Tenancies Act, 2006.

mock_statutes.text: 95(1) A tenant may assign a rental unit to another person with the consent of the landlord. A clause requiring consent that shall not be unreasonably withheld is fully compliant with this section.

test_cases.description: Auto-renew for new fixed term unless 60 days notice — unlawful renewal obligation
test_cases.clause_text: This tenancy will automatically renew for a further one-year fixed term at the end of the initial term unless the Tenant provides 60 days written notice of intention not to renew.

mock_statutes.text: 38(1) If a tenancy agreement for a fixed term expires and is not renewed and the tenant continues to occupy the rental unit, the tenancy shall be deemed to be renewed as a monthly tenancy on the same terms and conditions that are in the expired tenancy agreement and at the same rent that is in effect at the end of the fixed term. A fixed-term auto-renewal clause is void — the tenancy continues month-to-month, not as a new fixed term.

test_cases.description: Explicit month-to-month continuation at end of fixed term — compliant [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: At the end of the fixed term, this tenancy will continue on a month-to-month basis on the same terms and conditions, in accordance with section 38 of the Residential Tenancies Act, 2006.

mock_statutes.text: 38(1) If a fixed-term tenancy expires and the tenant continues to occupy, the tenancy continues as a monthly tenancy. A clause that explicitly states this month-to-month continuation is compliant and provides helpful clarity.

test_cases.description: Quarterly rent increases tied to CPI — multiple increases per 12 months, void under s.119
test_cases.clause_text: The rent shall be adjusted quarterly in accordance with the Consumer Price Index (CPI). On January 1st, April 1st, July 1st, and October 1st each year, rent will increase by the CPI percentage for the preceding quarter.

mock_statutes.text: 119(1) A landlord who is lawfully entitled to increase the rent charged to a tenant for a rental unit may do so only if at least 12 months have elapsed since the day of the last rent increase for that tenant in that rental unit. Only one rent increase is permitted per 12-month period. Quarterly or more frequent rent increases are void regardless of the amount or index used.

test_cases.description: Annual rent increase with guideline and 90 days notice — compliant [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: The Landlord may increase rent once per year in accordance with the Ontario Rent Increase Guideline. The Landlord will provide at least 90 days written notice on Form N1 before any increase takes effect.

mock_statutes.text: 119(1) A landlord may increase rent only if at least 12 months have elapsed since the last increase. An annual increase complying with the guideline is fully compliant with this provision.

test_cases.description: Landlord may remove included parking without rent reduction — void under RTA s.121/125
test_cases.clause_text: Included parking and storage are provided as a courtesy. The Landlord reserves the right to remove, reassign, or discontinue these services at any time without prior notice and without any reduction in rent.

mock_statutes.text: 125(1) A landlord and tenant may agree to a reduction in rent at any time. If a landlord reduces or discontinues a service or facility that was included in rent (such as parking or laundry), the tenant may apply to the Board for a rent reduction. A clause purporting to let the landlord remove included services without reducing rent is void under the Act.

test_cases.description: Included parking with explicit rent-reduction obligation — compliant [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: One underground parking space (P1-45) is included in the monthly rent. If the Landlord discontinues this service, the Landlord will reduce the monthly rent by $150 or the amount determined by the Landlord and Tenant Board.

mock_statutes.text: 125 A landlord and tenant may agree to a reduction in rent at any time. A clause that explicitly provides for a rent reduction if included services are removed is compliant with the RTA.

test_cases.description: Waiver of right to file LTB application — retaliation/coercion, void under RTA s.139/s.3
test_cases.clause_text: By signing this agreement, the Tenant waives any right to file a complaint, application, or claim against the Landlord with the Landlord and Tenant Board or any government authority for any reason whatsoever.

mock_statutes.text: 139 No person shall knowingly attempt, directly or indirectly, to coerce, compel or intimidate a tenant to waive their rights under this Act. Any waiver of the right to apply to the Landlord and Tenant Board is void under this section and under section 3(1) which provides that the Act applies despite any agreement to the contrary.
3(1) This Act applies despite any agreement or waiver to the contrary. A clause waiving the tenant's right to access the LTB is void under this provision.

test_cases.description: Both-party LTB access confirmed — compliant dispute resolution [FALSE POSITIVE GUARD]
test_cases.clause_text: Any disputes arising from this tenancy agreement shall be resolved in accordance with the Residential Tenancies Act, 2006. Both parties retain their full right to apply to the Landlord and Tenant Board.

mock_statutes.text: 139 No person shall coerce or intimidate a tenant to waive their rights under this Act. A clause explicitly confirming both parties' right to access the LTB is compliant and model language.

test_cases.description: Edge case: tenant pays hydro directly to utility — compliant, not vital services cutoff
test_cases.clause_text: The Tenant shall pay for hydro consumed in the rental unit directly to Toronto Hydro. Heat and water are included in the rent and provided by the Landlord.

mock_statutes.text: 29(1) A landlord shall not cause the supply of a vital service to be withheld or interrupted. It is permissible for a tenant to pay a utility bill directly to the provider — the key is that the landlord cannot cause the service to be cut off. Heat and water remaining landlord-provided satisfies the Act.
